summary: Hardened the RevenueCat/paywall surfaces so they are safe under Apple review while keeping Lullaby local-only v1. The primary defense was already in place: Pro ships OFF by default (EXPO_PUBLIC_PRO_ENABLED=0 in .env.example), so no paywall/upgrade card is reachable at all in the shipping build. The remaining risk was the defensive 'unconfigured' state (a build that enables Pro without keys/products, or where the native module is absent): the paywall could be reached but its Restore control was a permanently DISABLED dead-end, and restorePurchases() would have called an unconfigured SDK. I made Restore reachable from every paywall state and crash-safe. (1) src/state/ProProvider.tsx: restorePurchases() now short-circuits with a calm message ('Subscriptions are not available in this version yet.') and a coarse restore_failed{errorCode:'not_configured'} event when isRevenueCatConfigured() is false, so it never calls into an unconfigured/absent SDK. (2) src/components/pro/PaywallSheet.tsx: the Restore button is now tappable in every non-Pro state (disabled only while a restore is in flight) instead of being greyed out when unconfigured/signed-out; the canRestore flag now only drives the helper copy, and the restore hint copy was reworded to pair with an always-tappable button. The existing calm 'Subscriptions are not configured in this build yet.' fallback NoticeBox is preserved. No prices, no external payment links, no direct SDK import were added; the SDK stays isolated in src/lib/revenueCat.ts (all its calls are already try/catch-guarded to safe values). No live paywall was enabled and the bundle id was untouched.
validation_notes: npx tsc --noEmit → exit 0 (clean). npm run lint (expo lint) → exit 0 (clean). npm run check:local-interactions → All 388 checks passed (added RE7 pinning the reachable+crash-safe restore behavior; the prior 387 all still pass, including X1-X8, Z1-Z7, RE1-RE6 which constrain the paywall copy, the canRestore token, and the 'unconfigured' resolution). Verified statically that: with Pro OFF (default) no paywall entry point renders; with Pro enabled+no keys, ProProvider resolves paywallStatus='unconfigured' and Restore short-circuits without an SDK call.
manual_followups: Before ever enabling a live paywall (EXPO_PUBLIC_PRO_ENABLED=1 + real EXPO_PUBLIC_REVENUECAT_*_API_KEY), confirm the RevenueCat dashboard offering/entitlement ids match the code defaults (offering='default', entitlement='pro') and that App Store Connect subscription products are Approved/Ready — see docs/release-env.md. The current App Store submission should ship with Pro OFF.

Apple review 3.1.2 metadata: if Pro is later enabled, add links to the subscription Terms (EULA) and Privacy Policy on/near the paywall and in App Store Connect metadata; the paywall intentionally carries no external URLs today.

Do a device pass of the paywall in a real Pro-enabled build once products exist: purchase, restore, and the unconfigured-degrade path (offering fetch returns nothing) to confirm the calm states render as expected.
apple_review_risk_remaining: None for the intended local-only v1 submission, which ships with Pro OFF so no purchase/paywall surface is reachable.

If a build is mistakenly shipped with Pro ENABLED but products not production-ready, the paywall would be reachable showing the calm 'not configured' state with a Restore that reports 'not available' — non-crashing, but a reviewer could still flag a non-functional paywall; the guard against this remains configuration discipline (Pro OFF), documented in docs/release-env.md.
